response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with two decades of mostly web and full-stack experience, but he misses the role's hard requirements: there is no indication of Japanese proficiency (native level is mandatory), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and his C work is limited to a brief stint in 2007-2008. He shows no exposure to L2/L3 networking, switch platforms, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X, making him a poor fit for embedded LAN-switch security firmware development despite his otherwise strong engineering background.
strengths: 20+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded work
Low-latency systems programming experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers) handling millions of requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, test tooling authorship, and community teaching
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed; the role requires native-level Japanese
C for UNIX experience is minimal and dated (~6 months in 2007-2008); recent work is TypeScript/Node.js
No experience with Linux kernel internals, L2/L3 networking, switch hardware, or security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption)

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
